text: As artificial intelligence becomes more common in our daily lives, questions about ethics have grown more important. One major concern is bias: if an AI system learns from data that contains unfair patterns, it may make discriminatory decisions. For example, hiring software could favour certain groups over others. This is why fairness must be a priority; developers need to check their data and models regularly. Another key issue is privacy. AI tools often collect personal information, so users should know how their data is used. Transparency is also essential—people need to understand how decisions are made. Ultimately, responsible AI development means building systems that are safe, fair, and accountable to society.
